mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) relative to baseline during rate cuts, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to separate bull/bear outcomes with an accuracy above 52% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength that sustains corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in fewer than 45% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the index of industrial production reflects weakening manufacturing output that compresses profit margins, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive during industrial production contractions within tightening regimes, or the mechanism predictive power vanishes when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid month-over-month acceleration in consumer prices above 0.4% forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of high-inflation months, or the signal Sharpe ratio drops below 0.1 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and a simultaneously falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market absorption that supports consumer spending and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces negative forward returns in > 50% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Contraction in real GDP growth combined with declining retail sales indicates a synchronized demand shock that forces earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered quarters, or the signal fails to predict direction with an accuracy exceeding 53% during confirmed NBER recession periods.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in industrial production signal strengthening supply-side capacity that improves corporate inventory turnover and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no directional bias during industrial production expansions, or the Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested rolling windows.
